key_insight: ILLUME+ is a scalable explainability framework that goes beyond single-gene rankings to capture coordinated gene activity, yielding more stable and biologically informative explanations of cancer drug response.
clinical_relevance: Improved explainability in drug response models can help precision oncology researchers generate stronger hypotheses about mechanisms of sensitivity and resistance.
method: Post-hoc explainability integrated into an end-to-end transcriptomic drug-response prediction pipeline.
Produces multiple complementary explanation types instead of only univariate gene importance scores.
Designed to be scalable and more robust than existing explanation baselines.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that ILLUME+ produced more stable gene-importance scores than baselines and recovered known drug-gene associations, mechanisms of action, and novel interaction-driven signals.
watch_item: Even with improved stability, post-hoc explanations can still reflect model-specific patterns rather than direct biological causation.
paper_title: Explainable AI for Cancer Drug Response Prediction: Beyond Univariate Feature Attributions
